delivery.raw: オンライン同期型のみ

rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary:
This course introduces methods of comparative analysis applicable to research on local societies. The course consists of two parts. The first part deals with actual examples of comparative analysis on various issues of local societies. The second part is an exercise of comparative analyses using data of cross-national social survey. Students are required to learn methods of social survey data analyses, such as cross-tabulation analysis, correlation analysis, ANOVA, and regression analysis. They are also supposed to acquire other advanced methods depending on their research interest and necessities (e.g. multiple comparison, factor analysis, PCA, correspondence analysis, clustering, etc.)
Note 1: This course is designated as course I "Graduate Seminar in Multivariate Analyses" in Advanced Social Researcher curriculum by Japanese Association for Social Research.
Note 2: Reading materials, survey data, and statistical application to be used in this class are (almost) ALL in English.
aims: This course aims at offering methods of comparative analysis on societies, based on undergraduate level of social research and statistics.
goals: To comprehend macro-level comparative analyses written in English
To conduct micro-level comparative analysis by using statistical package
To make out a report of comparative analysis

plan.text: Introduction: Data-based Comparative Research of Local Societies
Macro-data Approach(2) How to Find Data
Micro-data Approach (3) Examples of Data-based Research
Macro-data Approach (4) How to Analyze Data and Read the Result
Micro-data Approach (2) How to Analyze Data: Basic Statistics, Bivariate Analyses
Micro-data Approach (3) How to Analyze Data: Basic of Regression Analysis
Micro-data Approach (4) How to Analyze Data: Applied Regression Analysis
Micro-data Approach (5) How to Analyze Data: Advanced Multivariate Methods
Analysis of Cross-national Social Survey Data (1) Selecting Countries and Regions to Be Analyzed
Analysis of Cross-national Social Survey Data (2) Building an Analytical Model
Analysis of Cross-national Social Survey Data (3) Conducting Multivariate Analyses
Analysis of Cross-national Social Survey Data (4) Drafting Report of Analyses
Analysis of Cross-national Social Survey Data (5) Reporting Result

textbooks: OECD (2024), OECD Regions and Cities at a Glance 2024, OECD Publishing, Paris, https://doi.org/10.1787/f42db3bf-en.
prereq: As was mentioned in the summary, using English is indispensable in this class.
prep: In the first half of the course students are required to read the material, as well as to report the parts allotted to them. In the second half the students will prepare their final report based on their own cross-national analysis. In order to do that, they have to learn how to use statistical software, to conduct analysis, and to develop a discussion based on the result of the analysis.
